assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as admission webhook compatibility issue post-upgrade and explains webhook is failing to work with new API version

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides specific kubectl commands to find cert-manager webhooks: 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' and 'kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager'

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides immediate fix by patching webhook configurations to bypass webhook functionality temporarily

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Does not provide kubectl delete commands for webhook configurations; only shows patching commands to change failurePolicy

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Explicitly warns that temporary failurePolicy: Ignore 'bypasses certificate validation entirely' and emphasizes it's temporary

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes specific command to check current cert-manager version and references compatibility documentation

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Provides specific upgrade commands for cert-manager to v1.15.0 (though expectation mentions 1.30, response addresses 1.32 compatibility which is correct for the scenario)

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Primary mitigation strategy shown is patching failurePolicy to 'Ignore' as alternative to deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides test commands: 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it --restart=Never -- echo' to verify pod creation works

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Does not explicitly mention persisting changes in source of truth like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Explicitly shows reverting failurePolicy back to 'Fail' after permanent fix and warns about security implications of Ignore policy
